# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUF Fact - Dual-write fact-file primitives
#
# Writes fact-files to both BURD_OUTPUT_DIR and BURD_TEMP_DIR.
# BURD_OUTPUT_DIR is cleared on next dispatch (latest-command convenience).
# BURD_TEMP_DIR is durable (addressable by remote consumers).

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard (return 0 — sourced from non-BCG dispatch context)
test -z "${ZBUF_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUF_SOURCED=1

# Tinder constants (pure string literals — available at source time)
BUF_burx_env="burx.env"

# Write a named fact-file to both output and temp directories.
# Dies if either copy already exists (double-write indicates a bug).
# Args: <filename> <value>
buf_write_fact() {
  local -r z_filename="$1"
  local -r z_value="$2"
  local -r z_output_path="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_filename}"
  local -r z_temp_path="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${z_filename}"
  test ! -f "${z_output_path}" || { echo "FATAL: buf_write_fact: preexists in output dir: ${z_output_path}" >&2; return 1; }
  test ! -f "${z_temp_path}"   || { echo "FATAL: buf_write_fact: preexists in temp dir: ${z_temp_path}" >&2; return 1; }
  printf '%s\n' "${z_value}" > "${z_output_path}"
  printf '%s\n' "${z_value}" > "${z_temp_path}"
}

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURH Regime - Bash Utility Regime Host Module
#
# BURH is a multi-instance regime — one file per SSH connection profile.
# Each profile lives at .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burh.env

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURH_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burh multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURH_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburh_*)

zburh_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURH_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burh already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all BURH variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURH

  buv_group_enroll "Connection Identity"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_HOST        1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote machine"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURH_USER        1   32  "Username on the remote host"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURH_ALIAS       1   64  "SSH alias and key filename (must match directory name)"

  buv_group_enroll "Authentication"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_SSH_PUBKEY  20 1024  "Full public key line (e.g., ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_KEY_FILE     0  256  "SSH private key filename in ~/.ssh/ (empty defaults to alias)"

  buv_group_enroll "Session Routing"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_COMMAND      0  512  "Shell command for command= routing in authorized_keys (empty for direct shell)"

  # Guard against unexpected BURH_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURH BURH_

  # Lock all enrolled BURH_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURH

  readonly ZBURH_KINDLED=1
}

zburh_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURH_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burh not kindled - call zburh_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURH enrollment validations
zburh_enforce() {
  zburh_sentinel

  buv_vet BURH
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (burh_*)

# Build a BURH authorized_keys line with alias marker.
# Pure string builder — no I/O. Caller decides how to write.
# Sets ZBURH_KEY_LINE (the full line) and ZBURH_KEY_MARKER (the grep tag).
# Prerequisite: BURH kindled (needs BURH_ALIAS, BURH_COMMAND, BURH_SSH_PUBKEY)
zburh_build_key_line() {
  zburh_sentinel

  ZBURH_KEY_MARKER="# BURH:${BURH_ALIAS}"

  if test -n "${BURH_COMMAND}"; then
    ZBURH_KEY_LINE="command=\"${BURH_COMMAND}\" ${BURH_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURH_KEY_MARKER}"
  else
    ZBURH_KEY_LINE="${BURH_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURH_KEY_MARKER}"
  fi
}

# List available alias names as space-separated tokens
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (needs BURS_USER), BURD kindled (needs BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burh_list_capture() {
  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}"
  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1

  local z_result=""
  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burh.env)
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_dir##*/}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

# eof

44	burh_render() {

46	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
47	
48	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
49	  buv_render BURH "BURH - Bash Utility Host Regime"
50	}
51	
52	burh_list() {
53	  buc_doc_brief "List available BURH host profiles for current user"
54	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
55	
56	  local z_aliases
57	  z_aliases=$(burh_list_capture) || buc_die "No BURH profiles found for user: ${BURS_USER}"
58	  buc_step "Available profiles for ${BURS_USER}:"
59	  local z_alias=""
60	  for z_alias in ${z_aliases}; do
61	    buc_bare "        ${z_alias}"
62	  done
63	}
64	
65	######################################################################
66	# Internal: Constructor Helper
67	#
68	# Shared logic for all platform constructors.
69	# Args: host user moniker suffix command_value key_file
70	# command_value and key_file may be empty.
71	
72	zburh_construct() {
73	  local z_host="${1:-}"
74	  local z_user="${2:-}"
75	  local z_moniker="${3:-}"
76	  local z_suffix="${4:-}"
77	  local z_command="${5:-}"
78	  local z_key_file="${6:-}"
79	
80	  test -n "${z_host}"    || buc_die "host required (arg 1)"
81	  test -n "${z_user}"    || buc_die "user required (arg 2)"
82	  test -n "${z_moniker}" || buc_die "moniker required (arg 3)"
83	
84	  local -r z_alias="${z_moniker}-${z_suffix}"
85	  local -r z_profile_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}"
86	  local -r z_profile_file="${z_profile_dir}/burh.env"
87	  local -r z_pubkey_file="${HOME}/.ssh/${z_alias}.pub"
88	  local z_pubkey=""
89	  local z_has_pubkey=0
90	
91	  if test -f "${z_pubkey_file}"; then
92	    z_pubkey=$(head -1 "${z_pubkey_file}")
93	    z_has_pubkey=1
94	    buc_step "Found public key: ${z_pubkey_file}"
95	  else
96	    z_pubkey="ssh-ed25519 REPLACE_WITH_ACTUAL_PUBKEY ${BURS_USER}@$(hostname -s)"
97	  fi
98	
99	  mkdir -p "${z_profile_dir}"
100	
101	  printf '%s\n' "BURH_HOST=${z_host}"           >  "${z_profile_file}"
102	  printf '%s\n' "BURH_USER=${z_user}"            >> "${z_profile_file}"
103	  printf '%s\n' "BURH_ALIAS=${z_alias}"          >> "${z_profile_file}"
104	  printf '%s\n' "BURH_SSH_PUBKEY='${z_pubkey}'"  >> "${z_profile_file}"
105	  printf '%s\n' "BURH_KEY_FILE=${z_key_file}"    >> "${z_profile_file}"
106	  printf '%s\n' "BURH_COMMAND='${z_command}'"    >> "${z_profile_file}"
107	
108	  buc_step "BURH profile written: ${z_profile_file}"
109	
110	  if test "${z_has_pubkey}" = "0"; then
111	    buh_e
112	    buh_section "SSH Key Required"
113	    buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_pubkey_file}"; local -r z_pubkey_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
114	    buh_line "No public key at ${z_pubkey_yelp}."
115	    buh_line   "Generate one:"
116	    buh_e
117	    buh_code   "ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/${z_alias}"
118	    buh_e
119	    buh_line   "Then re-run this constructor to populate the public key."
120	  fi
121	}
122	
123	######################################################################
124	# Command Functions — Constructors
125	
126	burh_construct_linux() {
127	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Linux target"
128	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Linux machine"
129	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
130	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-linux)"
131	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
132	
133	  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "linux" ""
134	}
135	
136	burh_construct_mac() {
137	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for macOS target"
138	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Mac"
139	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
140	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-mac)"
141	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
142	
143	  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "mac" ""
144	}
145	
146	burh_construct_cygwin() {
147	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Windows Cygwin target"
148	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
149	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
150	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-cyg)"
151	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
152	
153	  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "cyg" 'C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l'
154	}
155	
156	burh_construct_wsl() {
157	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Windows WSL target"
158	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
159	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
160	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-wsl)"
161	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
162	
163	  local -r z_wsl_distro="Ubuntu"
164	  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "wsl" "C:\\Windows\\System32\\wsl.exe -d ${z_wsl_distro}"
165	}
166	
167	burh_construct_powershell() {
168	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Windows PowerShell target"
169	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
170	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
171	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-ps)"
172	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
173	
174	  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "ps" 'C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe'
175	}
176	
177	burh_construct_localhost() {
178	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for localhost (local SSH)"
179	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on this machine"
180	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-local)"
181	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
182	
183	  zburh_construct "localhost" "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "local" "" "id_ed25519"
184	}
185	
186	######################################################################
187	# Command Functions — SSH Automation
188	
189	burh_ssh_config() {
190	  buc_doc_brief "Generate SSH config entries from all BURH profiles"
191	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
192	
193	  local z_aliases
194	  z_aliases=$(burh_list_capture) || buc_die "No BURH profiles found for user: ${BURS_USER}"
195	
196	  local -r z_config="${HOME}/.ssh/config"
197	  local -r z_begin="# --- BEGIN BURH MANAGED ---"
198	  local -r z_end="# --- END BURH MANAGED ---"
199	
200	  # Build managed section content
201	  local z_section=""
202	  z_section="${z_begin}"$'\n'
203	  z_section+="# Generated by buw-HWsc — do not edit this section manually"$'\n'
204	
205	  local z_alias=""
206	  for z_alias in ${z_aliases}; do
207	    local z_profile="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}/burh.env"
208	    # Source directly — BURH not kindled, no readonly conflict
209	    source "${z_profile}"
210	    z_section+=$'\n'
211	    local z_key="${BURH_KEY_FILE:-${BURH_ALIAS}}"
212	    z_section+="Host ${BURH_ALIAS}"$'\n'
213	    z_section+="  HostName ${BURH_HOST}"$'\n'
214	    z_section+="  User ${BURH_USER}"$'\n'
215	    z_section+="  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/${z_key}"$'\n'
216	  done
217	
218	  z_section+="${z_end}"$'\n'
219	
220	  # Write managed section into config
221	  mkdir -p "${HOME}/.ssh"
222	
223	  if test -f "${z_config}" && grep -q "${z_begin}" "${z_config}"; then
224	    # Replace existing managed section
225	    local -r z_temp=$(mktemp)
226	    local z_in_managed=0
227	    while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
228	      if test "${z_line}" = "${z_begin}"; then
229	        z_in_managed=1
230	        printf '%s' "${z_section}" >> "${z_temp}"
231	        continue
232	      fi
233	      if test "${z_in_managed}" = "1"; then
234	        test "${z_line}" = "${z_end}" && z_in_managed=0
235	        continue
236	      fi
237	      printf '%s\n' "${z_line}" >> "${z_temp}"
238	    done < "${z_config}"
239	    mv "${z_temp}" "${z_config}"
240	  elif test -f "${z_config}"; then
241	    # Append managed section
242	    printf '\n%s' "${z_section}" >> "${z_config}"
243	  else
244	    # Create new config
245	    printf '%s' "${z_section}" > "${z_config}"
246	  fi
247	
248	  chmod 600 "${z_config}"
249	  buc_step "SSH config updated: ${z_config}"
250	  buc_step "Managed profiles: ${z_aliases}"
251	}
252	
253	burh_verify_ssh() {
254	  buc_doc_brief "Verify SSH connectivity to a BURH profile"
255	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
256	
257	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
258	
259	  buc_step "Testing SSH to ${BURH_ALIAS} (${BURH_HOST})..."
260	  local z_result=""
261	  if z_result=$(ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=10 "${BURH_ALIAS}" whoami 2>&1); then
262	    buc_success "SSH OK: ${BURH_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
263	  else
264	    buc_die "SSH FAILED: ${BURH_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
265	  fi
266	}
267	
268	burh_install_key() {
269	  buc_doc_brief "Install BURH profile SSH key into authorized_keys (idempotent)"
270	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
271	
272	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
273	
274	  local -r z_auth_keys="${HOME}/.ssh/authorized_keys"
275	
276	  zburh_build_key_line
277	
278	  mkdir -p "${HOME}/.ssh"
279	
280	  if test -f "${z_auth_keys}"; then
281	    local -r z_temp=$(mktemp)
282	    grep -v "${ZBURH_KEY_MARKER}" "${z_auth_keys}" > "${z_temp}" || true
283	    mv "${z_temp}" "${z_auth_keys}"
284	  fi
285	
286	  printf '%s\n' "${ZBURH_KEY_LINE}" >> "${z_auth_keys}"
287	  chmod 600 "${z_auth_keys}"
288	
289	  buc_success "Key installed for ${BURH_ALIAS} in ${z_auth_keys}"
290	}
291	
292	######################################################################
293	# Furnish and Main
294	
295	zburh_furnish() {
296	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
297	  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
298	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
299	
300	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
301	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
302	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
303	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
304	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burh_regime.sh"
305	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
306	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"
307	
308	  zbuv_kindle
309	  zburd_kindle
310	  zburd_enforce
311	
312	  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"
313	
314	  zburc_kindle
315	  zburc_enforce
316	
317	  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"
318	
319	  zburs_kindle
320	  zburs_enforce
321	
322	  zbupr_kindle
323	
324	  # If BUZ_FOLIO is set, load and kindle the specified profile
325	  if test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"; then
326	    local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burh.env"
327	    test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "BURH profile not found: ${z_profile_file}"
328	    source "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURH: ${z_profile_file}"
329	    zburh_kindle
330	    zburh_enforce
331	  fi
332	}
333	
334	buc_execute burh_ "Bash Utility Host Regime" zburh_furnish "$@"
335	
336	# eof
337

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURH CLI - Command line interface for BURH host regime operations
#
# Four command families:
#   burh_validate/render/list  — regime read operations (require BUZ_FOLIO)
#   burh_construct_*           — profile constructors (write burh.env)
#   burh_ssh_*/burh_verify_*   — SSH automation (config, verify)
#   burh_install_key           — authorized_keys management (per-profile, idempotent)

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# Command Functions — Regime Operations

burh_validate() {
  buc_doc_brief "Validate BURH host regime profile via enrollment report"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
  buc_step "Validating BURH host regime"
  buv_report BURH "Host Regime"
  buc_step "BURH host regime valid"
}

burh_render() {
  buc_doc_brief "Display diagnostic view of BURH host regime profile"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
  buv_render BURH "BURH - Bash Utility Host Regime"
}

burh_list() {
  buc_doc_brief "List available BURH host profiles for current user"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_aliases
  z_aliases=$(burh_list_capture) || buc_die "No BURH profiles found for user: ${BURS_USER}"
  buc_step "Available profiles for ${BURS_USER}:"
  local z_alias=""
  for z_alias in ${z_aliases}; do
    buc_bare "        ${z_alias}"
  done
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Constructor Helper
#
# Shared logic for all platform constructors.
# Args: host user moniker suffix command_value key_file
# command_value and key_file may be empty.

zburh_construct() {
  local z_host="${1:-}"
  local z_user="${2:-}"
  local z_moniker="${3:-}"
  local z_suffix="${4:-}"
  local z_command="${5:-}"
  local z_key_file="${6:-}"

  test -n "${z_host}"    || buc_die "host required (arg 1)"
  test -n "${z_user}"    || buc_die "user required (arg 2)"
  test -n "${z_moniker}" || buc_die "moniker required (arg 3)"

  local -r z_alias="${z_moniker}-${z_suffix}"
  local -r z_profile_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}"
  local -r z_profile_file="${z_profile_dir}/burh.env"
  local -r z_pubkey_file="${HOME}/.ssh/${z_alias}.pub"
  local z_pubkey=""
  local z_has_pubkey=0

  if test -f "${z_pubkey_file}"; then
    z_pubkey=$(head -1 "${z_pubkey_file}")
    z_has_pubkey=1
    buc_step "Found public key: ${z_pubkey_file}"
  else
    z_pubkey="ssh-ed25519 REPLACE_WITH_ACTUAL_PUBKEY ${BURS_USER}@$(hostname -s)"
  fi

  mkdir -p "${z_profile_dir}"

  printf '%s\n' "BURH_HOST=${z_host}"           >  "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURH_USER=${z_user}"            >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURH_ALIAS=${z_alias}"          >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURH_SSH_PUBKEY='${z_pubkey}'"  >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURH_KEY_FILE=${z_key_file}"    >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURH_COMMAND='${z_command}'"    >> "${z_profile_file}"

  buc_step "BURH profile written: ${z_profile_file}"

  if test "${z_has_pubkey}" = "0"; then
    buh_e
    buh_section "SSH Key Required"
    buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_pubkey_file}"; local -r z_pubkey_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
    buh_line "No public key at ${z_pubkey_yelp}."
    buh_line   "Generate one:"
    buh_e
    buh_code   "ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/${z_alias}"
    buh_e
    buh_line   "Then re-run this constructor to populate the public key."
  fi
}

######################################################################
# Command Functions — Constructors

burh_construct_linux() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Linux target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Linux machine"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-linux)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "linux" ""
}

burh_construct_mac() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for macOS target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Mac"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-mac)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "mac" ""
}

burh_construct_cygwin() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Windows Cygwin target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-cyg)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "cyg" 'C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l'
}

burh_construct_wsl() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Windows WSL target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-wsl)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_wsl_distro="Ubuntu"
  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "wsl" "C:\\Windows\\System32\\wsl.exe -d ${z_wsl_distro}"
}

burh_construct_powershell() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for Windows PowerShell target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Windows machine"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the Windows host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-ps)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburh_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "ps" 'C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe'
}

burh_construct_localhost() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURH profile for localhost (local SSH)"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on this machine"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-local)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburh_construct "localhost" "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "local" "" "id_ed25519"
}

######################################################################
# Command Functions — SSH Automation

burh_ssh_config() {
  buc_doc_brief "Generate SSH config entries from all BURH profiles"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_aliases
  z_aliases=$(burh_list_capture) || buc_die "No BURH profiles found for user: ${BURS_USER}"

  local -r z_config="${HOME}/.ssh/config"
  local -r z_begin="# --- BEGIN BURH MANAGED ---"
  local -r z_end="# --- END BURH MANAGED ---"

  # Build managed section content
  local z_section=""
  z_section="${z_begin}"$'\n'
  z_section+="# Generated by buw-HWsc — do not edit this section manually"$'\n'

  local z_alias=""
  for z_alias in ${z_aliases}; do
    local z_profile="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}/burh.env"
    # Source directly — BURH not kindled, no readonly conflict
    source "${z_profile}"
    z_section+=$'\n'
    local z_key="${BURH_KEY_FILE:-${BURH_ALIAS}}"
    z_section+="Host ${BURH_ALIAS}"$'\n'
    z_section+="  HostName ${BURH_HOST}"$'\n'
    z_section+="  User ${BURH_USER}"$'\n'
    z_section+="  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/${z_key}"$'\n'
  done

  z_section+="${z_end}"$'\n'

  # Write managed section into config
  mkdir -p "${HOME}/.ssh"

  if test -f "${z_config}" && grep -q "${z_begin}" "${z_config}"; then
    # Replace existing managed section
    local -r z_temp=$(mktemp)
    local z_in_managed=0
    while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
      if test "${z_line}" = "${z_begin}"; then
        z_in_managed=1
        printf '%s' "${z_section}" >> "${z_temp}"
        continue
      fi
      if test "${z_in_managed}" = "1"; then
        test "${z_line}" = "${z_end}" && z_in_managed=0
        continue
      fi
      printf '%s\n' "${z_line}" >> "${z_temp}"
    done < "${z_config}"
    mv "${z_temp}" "${z_config}"
  elif test -f "${z_config}"; then
    # Append managed section
    printf '\n%s' "${z_section}" >> "${z_config}"
  else
    # Create new config
    printf '%s' "${z_section}" > "${z_config}"
  fi

  chmod 600 "${z_config}"
  buc_step "SSH config updated: ${z_config}"
  buc_step "Managed profiles: ${z_aliases}"
}

burh_verify_ssh() {
  buc_doc_brief "Verify SSH connectivity to a BURH profile"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"

  buc_step "Testing SSH to ${BURH_ALIAS} (${BURH_HOST})..."
  local z_result=""
  if z_result=$(ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=10 "${BURH_ALIAS}" whoami 2>&1); then
    buc_success "SSH OK: ${BURH_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
  else
    buc_die "SSH FAILED: ${BURH_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
  fi
}

burh_install_key() {
  buc_doc_brief "Install BURH profile SSH key into authorized_keys (idempotent)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"

  local -r z_auth_keys="${HOME}/.ssh/authorized_keys"

  zburh_build_key_line

  mkdir -p "${HOME}/.ssh"

  if test -f "${z_auth_keys}"; then
    local -r z_temp=$(mktemp)
    grep -v "${ZBURH_KEY_MARKER}" "${z_auth_keys}" > "${z_temp}" || true
    mv "${z_temp}" "${z_auth_keys}"
  fi

  printf '%s\n' "${ZBURH_KEY_LINE}" >> "${z_auth_keys}"
  chmod 600 "${z_auth_keys}"

  buc_success "Key installed for ${BURH_ALIAS} in ${z_auth_keys}"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zburh_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burh_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zburd_enforce

  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"

  zburc_kindle
  zburc_enforce

  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"

  zburs_kindle
  zburs_enforce

  zbupr_kindle

  # If BUZ_FOLIO is set, load and kindle the specified profile
  if test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"; then
    local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burh.env"
    test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "BURH profile not found: ${z_profile_file}"
    source "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURH: ${z_profile_file}"
    zburh_kindle
    zburh_enforce
  fi
}

buc_execute burh_ "Bash Utility Host Regime" zburh_furnish "$@"

# eof

**Heat**: rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (₣BB) — racing
**Pace**: buf-fact-multi-foundation (₢BBAAO) — rough, coding-only, BUK-domain
**Sibling running elsewhere**: BBAAM (depot-identity-collapse) — disjoint files, no conflict
**Downstream consumers**: BBAAP (multi-fact depot_list), BBAAN+downstream (observation-fact convention via cult-verb refactor)

## Name assessment

`buf-fact-multi-foundation` fits the docket cleanly. "buf-fact" names the module surface, "multi" names the new shape, "foundation" names the role. No rename.

## Current state of the surface

**`Tools/buk/buf_fact.sh`** (49 lines):
- One function: `buf_write_fact(filename, value)` — single-fact, dual-write to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` + `BURD_TEMP_DIR`, dies on preexistence
- One tinder constant: `BUF_burx_env="burx.env"`

**Existing single-fact callsites** (28 in tree, all stable per docket scope):
- `RBF_FACT_*` family (rbgc_Constants.sh:143-148) — already follows "value matches name downcased"
- `RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL` (rbgc_Constants.sh:151) — same shape
- `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT="depot"` (rbgc_Constants.sh:156) — bare extension, BBAAM-introduced, RB-domain (stays where it is per BUK/RB boundary)

**`Tools/buk/burh_regime.sh:96-112`** — `burh_list_capture` walks `${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/*/burh.env` and returns space-separated alias names; **`Tools/buk/burh_cli.sh:52-63`** — `burh_list` prints them via `buc_step` + `buc_bare` loop.

## Proposed approach

Three load-bearing changes settled in the paddock:

1. **`buf_write_fact_multi(file_root, ext, content)`** in `buf_fact.sh` — same dual-write shape, opaque `ext` pass-through, produces `<file_root>.<ext>`
2. **`BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias"`** constant in `buf_fact.sh` alongside the function — establishes the registry-is-the-file pattern with the discoverability convention (value matches name downcased)
3. **Per-alias emission** in BURH list path — one `<alias>.buf_ext_alias` fact per profile, empty content (presence is the fact)

## Open mount-time decisions

Worth deciding before code touches:

- **Rename `buf_write_fact` → `buf_write_fact_single`?** Docket leaves open. Lean: no — 28 stable callsites, the `_multi` suffix carries the distinction, no symmetry win worth churn.
- **Emit in `burh_list_capture` (regime walker) or `burh_list` (cli)?** Capture is currently pure (string return). Lean: emit in `burh_list` cli during the existing alias loop, keeps capture pure and emission a CLI concern.
- **Empty content semantics.** Existing `buf_write_fact` does `printf '%s\n' "${z_value}"` — empty string yields a one-byte newline file. Acceptable for "presence is the fact" but worth confirming. Alternative: tweak `_multi` to `printf '%s' "${z_value}"` (no trailing newline) so empty → zero-byte file.
- **Self-test coverage.** Existing `Tools/buk/buts/butcbx_BurxExchange.sh` covers `buf_write_fact` (15 cases). Lean: add multi-fact cases to butcbx (closest sibling) rather than spinning a new butc*-style file.

## Execution strategy

- **Model tier**: sonnet — BCG-compliant bash, paddock vocabulary settled, no architectural judgment
- **Parallelization**: sequential — single tight surface (one `.sh` for the function/registry, one `.sh` for the proof-of-shape, one self-test edit)
- **Coupling to BBAAM**: zero file overlap, but both committing to main concurrently — I'll keep file lists tight in `jjx_record` so the swim lanes stay clean

Ready to proceed when you decide on the four open mount-time questions. Want me to ask them as a sequence, or do you have answers already?

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUTCBX - BURX exchange test cases for BUK self-test
#
# Verifies that bud_dispatch.sh produces well-formed burx.env via
# dual-write during dispatch.  These cases inspect the current dispatch's
# own output — the dispatch IS the integration test for buf_write_fact.
# All tests are pure local — no GCP, no containers, no network.

set -euo pipefail

######################################################################
# Test cases — direct assertions inside zbute_tcase subshell

butcbx_burx_dual_write_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: burx.env exists in both output and temp dirs (dual-write)"

  test -f "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}"  || buto_fatal "Missing temp dir copy"
  test -f "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}" || buto_fatal "Missing output dir copy"

  local -r z_tmp_content=$(<"${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}")
  local -r z_out_content=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}")
  test "${z_tmp_content}" = "${z_out_content}" \
    || buto_fatal "Dual-write mismatch between output and temp dirs"
}

butcbx_burx_fields_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: burx.env is sourceable and has all initial fields"

  local -r z_burx="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}"

  # Verify sourceable in a subshell
  local z_status=0
  ( source "${z_burx}" ) || z_status=$?
  test "${z_status}" -eq 0 || buto_fatal "burx.env is not sourceable (status ${z_status})"

  # Source and check all initial fields
  source "${z_burx}"

  test -n "${BURX_PID:-}"        || buto_fatal "BURX_PID missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_BEGAN_AT:-}"   || buto_fatal "BURX_BEGAN_AT missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_TABTARGET:-}"  || buto_fatal "BURX_TABTARGET missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_TEMP_DIR:-}"   || buto_fatal "BURX_TEMP_DIR missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_TRANSCRIPT:-}" || buto_fatal "BURX_TRANSCRIPT missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_LOG_HIST:-}"   || buto_fatal "BURX_LOG_HIST missing or empty"

  # BURX_LABEL may be empty — verify it is declared (not missing)
  local -r z_label_check="${BURX_LABEL+declared}"
  test "${z_label_check}" = "declared" || buto_fatal "BURX_LABEL not declared in burx.env"
}

butcbx_burx_preexist_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: buf_write_fact rejects preexisting file"

  local -r z_stderr="${BUT_TEMP_DIR}/preexist_stderr.txt"

  # burx.env already exists from this dispatch's initial write
  local z_status=0
  buf_write_fact "${BUF_burx_env}" "duplicate-write" 2>"${z_stderr}" || z_status=$?
  test "${z_status}" -ne 0 \
    || buto_fatal "buf_write_fact should have failed on preexisting burx.env"
}

butcbx_burx_timestamp_format_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: BURX_BEGAN_AT matches nanosecond timestamp format"

  source "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}"

  # Format: YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN
  local -r z_pattern='^[0-9]{8}-[0-9]{6}\.[0-9]{9}$'
  [[ "${BURX_BEGAN_AT}" =~ ${z_pattern} ]] \
    || buto_fatal "BURX_BEGAN_AT format invalid: '${BURX_BEGAN_AT}' (expected YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN)"
}

# eof

buc_step 'Grant roles/owner on depot project'
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role \
    "${z_token}" \
    "Grant Governor Owner" \
    "projects/${z_depot_project_id}" \
    "roles/owner" \
    "serviceAccount:${z_governor_email}" \
    "governor-owner"

  buc_step 'Generate service account key (with propagation retry)'
  local -r z_key_req="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_governor_key_request.json"
  printf '%s' '{"privateKeyType": "TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE"}' > "${z_key_req}"

  local -r z_key_url="${z_sa_list_url}/${z_governor_email}/keys"
  local z_key_attempt=0
  local z_gov_key_infix=""
  local z_key_code=""
  while :; do
    z_key_attempt=$((z_key_attempt + 1))

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUF Fact - Dual-write fact-file primitives
#
# Writes fact-files to both BURD_OUTPUT_DIR and BURD_TEMP_DIR.
# BURD_OUTPUT_DIR is cleared on next dispatch (latest-command convenience).
# BURD_TEMP_DIR is durable (addressable by remote consumers).

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard (return 0 — sourced from non-BCG dispatch context)
test -z "${ZBUF_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUF_SOURCED=1

# Tinder constants (pure string literals — available at source time)
BUF_burx_env="burx.env"

# Multi-fact extension registry (BUK-domain only). Constant value matches
# constant name downcased; the buf_ext_ prefix in the value carries the
# owning module identity so any fact file is recognizable on disk.
BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias"

# Write a named fact-file to both output and temp directories.
# Dies if either copy already exists (double-write indicates a bug).
# Args: <filename> <value>
buf_write_fact_single() {
  local -r z_filename="$1"
  local -r z_value="$2"
  local -r z_output_path="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_filename}"
  local -r z_temp_path="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${z_filename}"
  test ! -f "${z_output_path}" || { echo "FATAL: buf_write_fact_single: preexists in output dir: ${z_output_path}" >&2; return 1; }
  test ! -f "${z_temp_path}"   || { echo "FATAL: buf_write_fact_single: preexists in temp dir: ${z_temp_path}" >&2; return 1; }
  printf '%s\n' "${z_value}" > "${z_output_path}"
  printf '%s\n' "${z_value}" > "${z_temp_path}"
}

# Write a multi-element fact-file (<file_root>.<ext>) to both output and
# temp directories. Dies if either copy already exists. Extension is an
# opaque pass-through — no validation, no closed-set enforcement.
# Args: <file_root> <ext> <value>
buf_write_fact_multi() {
  local -r z_root="$1"
  local -r z_ext="$2"
  local -r z_value="$3"
  local -r z_filename="${z_root}.${z_ext}"
  local -r z_output_path="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_filename}"
  local -r z_temp_path="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${z_filename}"
  test ! -f "${z_output_path}" || { echo "FATAL: buf_write_fact_multi: preexists in output dir: ${z_output_path}" >&2; return 1; }
  test ! -f "${z_temp_path}"   || { echo "FATAL: buf_write_fact_multi: preexists in temp dir: ${z_temp_path}" >&2; return 1; }
  printf '%s\n' "${z_value}" > "${z_output_path}"
  printf '%s\n' "${z_value}" > "${z_temp_path}"
}

# eof

content.content:
M Tools/buk/buf_fact.sh
---HEAD---
b010808d jjb:1011-b7b418b1:₢BBAAM:n: Step 3 of rbrr-depot-identity-collapse: rbgp_depot_unmake reshape to zero-arg, RBDC-driven, mirroring Step 2's levy treatment. (1) CLI positional arg dropped: 'local -r z_depot_project_id="${1:-}"' and 'buc_doc_param "depot_project_id" ...' both removed. Function now takes its target from kindled regime via ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}. (2) Dead validation triplet dropped: the 'test -n "${z_depot_project_id}" || buc_die "... (current derived: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID})"' under 'Safety confirmation required' (zero-arg can never be empty), the duplicate 'test -n "${z_depot_project_id}"' under 'Validate target depot' (same), and the RBRP env-var pair 'test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}"' / 'test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}"' under 'Authenticate as Payor' — same belt-and-suspenders dead pattern killed in levy's Step 2 cleanup; zrbrp_enforce in zrbgp_furnish covers all three RBRP fields before any rbgp command runs. (3) Interactive confirmation reshape: 'buc_require "DANGER: Permanently destroy depot ${z_depot_project_id} and ALL resources" "${z_depot_project_id}"' switched to use ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID} for both the warning banner and the operator-must-type confirmation token. (4) URL/body z_depot_project_id sweep: 7 sites in unmake (z_project_info_url, z_liens_url, z_billing_unlink_url, z_tether_del_url, z_airgap_del_url, z_delete_url, the buc_success final-banner string) all switched to ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}. (5) Prefix-strip + timestamp-suffix arithmetic block dropped entirely: the four-line block reconstructing z_depot_name via 'z_without_prefix="${z_depot_project_id#"${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-"}"' + z_name_len + z_ts_suffix_len=$((1 + RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN)) + substring slice has no purpose post-collapse — there's no timestamp to strip and the moniker is available directly via RBRR. (6) z_pool_stem local dropped in favor of ${RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM} at both tether and airgap delete sites (mirrors levy's treatment in Step 2). Out-of-Step-3-scope (deliberately): zrbgp_depot_list_update call retained (rbgp_depot_list reshape lives in Step 4 territory per Step 1 commit's annotation); RBSDU-depot_unmake.adoc operation spec staleness parallel to RBSDE (separate pace material); tt/rbw-dU.PayorUnmakesDepot.sh tabtarget unchanged (standard zero-arg ${@} pattern, no asymmetry with peers). Not yet tested per user instruction — build/unit/fast-suite verification pending the next pass.
b7b418b1 jjb:1011-4a66a4ce:₢BBAAM:n: DRY the joint-length check in zrbrr_enforce. Prior commit had ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} repeated 4 times, ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} repeated 4 times, and the (prefix + 2 + moniker) sum computed inline twice (once in the test, once in the buc_die message). Factored to three local -r bindings: z_prefix_len, z_moniker_len, z_joint_len. Test and message now reference each computed value once. The 'd-' literal still appears once in the arithmetic and once in the message body — different roles (calculation vs operator-facing explanation), kept both. Build green; rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast 97/97.
4a66a4ce jjb:1011-ee816373:₢BBAAM:n: Step 2 follow-on cleanup of rbgp_depot_levy surfaced by post-edit review. (1) Auth/region consolidation: the 'Validate input parameters' wrapping step (renamed to 'Validate region against Artifact Registry locations' in the prior notch) authenticated once at line 578 to call the AR locations API for region validation, then the next step 'Authenticate as Payor' authenticated again at line 597 with a duplicate 'local z_token' declaration and identical zrbgp_authenticate_capture call — the first token was unused after the region check. Reordered: 'Authenticate as Payor' becomes step 1 with the single 'local z_token' capture, 'Validate region against Artifact Registry locations' becomes step 2 reusing that token. One auth, one token, dependency between the two steps now explicit (region-check requires authenticated AR access). (2) Dead RBRP env-var checks dropped: the three 'test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die ...' / RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID / RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID lines under the old 'Authenticate as Payor' step were belt-and-suspenders dead code — zrbrp_enforce in zrbgp_furnish already validates these are non-empty before any rbgp command runs (rbgp_cli.sh line 63), and Step 2 unconditionalizing zrbrr_enforce makes the analogous-pattern asymmetry stand out. Removed all three. (3) Joint-length validation added at the right layer: zrbrr_enforce (rbrr_regime.sh) gains a custom check after the CLOUD_PREFIX/RUNTIME_PREFIX format checks asserting $(( ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} + 2 + ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} )) -le 30, with a buc_die message naming each component's length and the joint sum so an operator can immediately see which knob to dial back. The previous inline 30-char guard in levy fired only after auth + region API + project body construction; the regime-kindle layer catches the failure before any GCP API call. Worst-case math under existing individual caps: CLOUD_PREFIX max 11 (incl. trailing hyphen) + 'd-'(2) + MONIKER max 26 = 39 chars; the joint cap excludes the 30..39 sliver from regime validity. P2 #4 (RBAGS rename) withdrawn after rechecking AXLA-Lexicon.adoc:51 — RBAGS is an active umbrella xref ('Recipe Bottle Admin Google Specification'), used consistently across three buc_doc_brief sites (payor_install, depot_levy, payor_refresh); not stale, leaving as-is. Build green: rbtd-b, rbtd-t (58/58), rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast (97/97). Existing fast-suite negative tests don't probe the new joint-length check (the rbrr-bad-cloud-prefix-too-long case fires on individual-length cap before joint sum matters); a future case 'rbrr-joint-length-too-long' would round out coverage but adds a Rust fixture surface, deferred.

#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUTCBX - BURX exchange test cases for BUK self-test
#
# Verifies that bud_dispatch.sh produces well-formed burx.env via
# dual-write during dispatch.  These cases inspect the current dispatch's
# own output — the dispatch IS the integration test for buf_write_fact.
# All tests are pure local — no GCP, no containers, no network.

set -euo pipefail

######################################################################
# Test cases — direct assertions inside zbute_tcase subshell

butcbx_burx_dual_write_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: burx.env exists in both output and temp dirs (dual-write)"

  test -f "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}"  || buto_fatal "Missing temp dir copy"
  test -f "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}" || buto_fatal "Missing output dir copy"

  local -r z_tmp_content=$(<"${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}")
  local -r z_out_content=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}")
  test "${z_tmp_content}" = "${z_out_content}" \
    || buto_fatal "Dual-write mismatch between output and temp dirs"
}

butcbx_burx_fields_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: burx.env is sourceable and has all initial fields"

  local -r z_burx="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}"

  # Verify sourceable in a subshell
  local z_status=0
  ( source "${z_burx}" ) || z_status=$?
  test "${z_status}" -eq 0 || buto_fatal "burx.env is not sourceable (status ${z_status})"

  # Source and check all initial fields
  source "${z_burx}"

  test -n "${BURX_PID:-}"        || buto_fatal "BURX_PID missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_BEGAN_AT:-}"   || buto_fatal "BURX_BEGAN_AT missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_TABTARGET:-}"  || buto_fatal "BURX_TABTARGET missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_TEMP_DIR:-}"   || buto_fatal "BURX_TEMP_DIR missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_TRANSCRIPT:-}" || buto_fatal "BURX_TRANSCRIPT missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_LOG_HIST:-}"   || buto_fatal "BURX_LOG_HIST missing or empty"

  # BURX_LABEL may be empty — verify it is declared (not missing)
  local -r z_label_check="${BURX_LABEL+declared}"
  test "${z_label_check}" = "declared" || buto_fatal "BURX_LABEL not declared in burx.env"
}

butcbx_burx_preexist_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: buf_write_fact rejects preexisting file"

  local -r z_stderr="${BUT_TEMP_DIR}/preexist_stderr.txt"

  # burx.env already exists from this dispatch's initial write
  local z_status=0
  buf_write_fact "${BUF_burx_env}" "duplicate-write" 2>"${z_stderr}" || z_status=$?
  test "${z_status}" -ne 0 \
    || buto_fatal "buf_write_fact should have failed on preexisting burx.env"
}

butcbx_burx_timestamp_format_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: BURX_BEGAN_AT matches nanosecond timestamp format"

  source "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}"

  # Format: YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN
  local -r z_pattern='^[0-9]{8}-[0-9]{6}\.[0-9]{9}$'
  [[ "${BURX_BEGAN_AT}" =~ ${z_pattern} ]] \
    || buto_fatal "BURX_BEGAN_AT format invalid: '${BURX_BEGAN_AT}' (expected YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN)"
}

# eof

b010808d jjb:1011-b7b418b1:₢BBAAM:n: Step 3 of rbrr-depot-identity-collapse: rbgp_depot_unmake reshape to zero-arg, RBDC-driven, mirroring Step 2's levy treatment. (1) CLI positional arg dropped: 'local -r z_depot_project_id="${1:-}"' and 'buc_doc_param "depot_project_id" ...' both removed. Function now takes its target from kindled regime via ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}. (2) Dead validation triplet dropped: the 'test -n "${z_depot_project_id}" || buc_die "... (current derived: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID})"' under 'Safety confirmation required' (zero-arg can never be empty), the duplicate 'test -n "${z_depot_project_id}"' under 'Validate target depot' (same), and the RBRP env-var pair 'test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}"' / 'test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}"' under 'Authenticate as Payor' — same belt-and-suspenders dead pattern killed in levy's Step 2 cleanup; zrbrp_enforce in zrbgp_furnish covers all three RBRP fields before any rbgp command runs. (3) Interactive confirmation reshape: 'buc_require "DANGER: Permanently destroy depot ${z_depot_project_id} and ALL resources" "${z_depot_project_id}"' switched to use ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID} for both the warning banner and the operator-must-type confirmation token. (4) URL/body z_depot_project_id sweep: 7 sites in unmake (z_project_info_url, z_liens_url, z_billing_unlink_url, z_tether_del_url, z_airgap_del_url, z_delete_url, the buc_success final-banner string) all switched to ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}. (5) Prefix-strip + timestamp-suffix arithmetic block dropped entirely: the four-line block reconstructing z_depot_name via 'z_without_prefix="${z_depot_project_id#"${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-"}"' + z_name_len + z_ts_suffix_len=$((1 + RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN)) + substring slice has no purpose post-collapse — there's no timestamp to strip and the moniker is available directly via RBRR. (6) z_pool_stem local dropped in favor of ${RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM} at both tether and airgap delete sites (mirrors levy's treatment in Step 2). Out-of-Step-3-scope (deliberately): zrbgp_depot_list_update call retained (rbgp_depot_list reshape lives in Step 4 territory per Step 1 commit's annotation); RBSDU-depot_unmake.adoc operation spec staleness parallel to RBSDE (separate pace material); tt/rbw-dU.PayorUnmakesDepot.sh tabtarget unchanged (standard zero-arg ${@} pattern, no asymmetry with peers). Not yet tested per user instruction — build/unit/fast-suite verification pending the next pass.
b7b418b1 jjb:1011-4a66a4ce:₢BBAAM:n: DRY the joint-length check in zrbrr_enforce. Prior commit had ${#RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} repeated 4 times, ${#RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} repeated 4 times, and the (prefix + 2 + moniker) sum computed inline twice (once in the test, once in the buc_die message). Factored to three local -r bindings: z_prefix_len, z_moniker_len, z_joint_len. Test and message now reference each computed value once. The 'd-' literal still appears once in the arithmetic and once in the message body — different roles (calculation vs operator-facing explanation), kept both. Build green; rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast 97/97.

(current derived: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID})"' under 'Safety confirmation required' (zero-arg can never be empty), the duplicate 'test -n "${z_depot_project_id}"' under 'Validate target depot' (same), and the RBRP env-var pair 'test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}"' / 'test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}"' under 'Authenticate as Payor' — same belt-and-suspenders dead pattern killed in levy's Step 2 cleanup; zrbrp_enforce in zrbgp_furnish covers all three RBRP fields before any rbgp command runs. (3) Interactive confirmation reshape: 'buc_require "DANGER: Permanently destroy depot ${z_depot_project_id} and ALL resources" "${z_depot_project_id}"' switched to use ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID} for both the warning banner and the operator-must-type confirmation token. (5) Prefix-strip + timestamp-suffix arithmetic block dropped entirely: the four-line block reconstructing z_depot_name via 'z_without_prefix="${z_depot_project_id#"${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT}-"}"' + z_name_len + z_ts_suffix_len=$((1 + RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN)) + substring slice has no purpose post-collapse — there's no timestamp to strip and the moniker is available directly via RBRR. (6) z_pool_stem local dropped in favor of ${RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM} at both tether and airgap delete sites (mirrors levy's treatment in Step 2). Out-of-Step-3-scope (deliberately): zrbgp_depot_list_update call retained (rbgp_depot_list reshape lives in Step 4 territory per Step 1 commit's annotation); RBSDU-depot_unmake.adoc operation spec staleness parallel to RBSDE (separate pace material); tt/rbw-dU.PayorUnmakesDepot.sh tabtarget unchanged (standard zero-arg ${@} pattern, no asymmetry with peers). # List available alias names as space-separated tokens
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (needs BURS_USER), BURD kindled (needs BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burh_list_capture() {
  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}"
  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1

  local z_result=""
  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burh.env)
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_dir##*/}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURH Regime - Bash Utility Regime Host Module
#
# BURH is a multi-instance regime — one file per SSH connection profile.
# Each profile lives at .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burh.env

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURH_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burh multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURH_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburh_*)

zburh_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURH_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burh already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all BURH variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURH

  buv_group_enroll "Connection Identity"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_HOST        1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote machine"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURH_USER        1   32  "Username on the remote host"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURH_ALIAS       1   64  "SSH alias and key filename (must match directory name)"

  buv_group_enroll "Authentication"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_SSH_PUBKEY  20 1024  "Full public key line (e.g., ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_KEY_FILE     0  256  "SSH private key filename in ~/.ssh/ (empty defaults to alias)"

  buv_group_enroll "Session Routing"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_COMMAND      0  512  "Shell command for command= routing in authorized_keys (empty for direct shell)"

  # Guard against unexpected BURH_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURH BURH_

  # Lock all enrolled BURH_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURH

  readonly ZBURH_KINDLED=1
}

zburh_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURH_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burh not kindled - call zburh_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURH enrollment validations
zburh_enforce() {
  zburh_sentinel

  buv_vet BURH
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (burh_*)

# Build a BURH authorized_keys line with alias marker.
# Pure string builder — no I/O. Caller decides how to write.
# Sets ZBURH_KEY_LINE (the full line) and ZBURH_KEY_MARKER (the grep tag).
# Prerequisite: BURH kindled (needs BURH_ALIAS, BURH_COMMAND, BURH_SSH_PUBKEY)
zburh_build_key_line() {
  zburh_sentinel

  ZBURH_KEY_MARKER="# BURH:${BURH_ALIAS}"

  if test -n "${BURH_COMMAND}"; then
    ZBURH_KEY_LINE="command=\"${BURH_COMMAND}\" ${BURH_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURH_KEY_MARKER}"
  else
    ZBURH_KEY_LINE="${BURH_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURH_KEY_MARKER}"
  fi
}

# List available alias names as space-separated tokens
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (needs BURS_USER), BURD kindled (needs BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burh_list_capture() {
  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}"
  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1

  local z_result=""
  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burh.env)
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_dir##*/}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUTCBX - BURX exchange test cases for BUK self-test
#
# Verifies that bud_dispatch.sh produces well-formed burx.env via
# dual-write during dispatch.  These cases inspect the current dispatch's
# own output — the dispatch IS the integration test for buf_write_fact_single.
# All tests are pure local — no GCP, no containers, no network.

set -euo pipefail

######################################################################
# Test cases — direct assertions inside zbute_tcase subshell

butcbx_burx_dual_write_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: burx.env exists in both output and temp dirs (dual-write)"

  test -f "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}"  || buto_fatal "Missing temp dir copy"
  test -f "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}" || buto_fatal "Missing output dir copy"

  local -r z_tmp_content=$(<"${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}")
  local -r z_out_content=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}")
  test "${z_tmp_content}" = "${z_out_content}" \
    || buto_fatal "Dual-write mismatch between output and temp dirs"
}

butcbx_burx_fields_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: burx.env is sourceable and has all initial fields"

  local -r z_burx="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}"

  # Verify sourceable in a subshell
  local z_status=0
  ( source "${z_burx}" ) || z_status=$?
  test "${z_status}" -eq 0 || buto_fatal "burx.env is not sourceable (status ${z_status})"

  # Source and check all initial fields
  source "${z_burx}"

  test -n "${BURX_PID:-}"        || buto_fatal "BURX_PID missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_BEGAN_AT:-}"   || buto_fatal "BURX_BEGAN_AT missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_TABTARGET:-}"  || buto_fatal "BURX_TABTARGET missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_TEMP_DIR:-}"   || buto_fatal "BURX_TEMP_DIR missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_TRANSCRIPT:-}" || buto_fatal "BURX_TRANSCRIPT missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_LOG_HIST:-}"   || buto_fatal "BURX_LOG_HIST missing or empty"

  # BURX_LABEL may be empty — verify it is declared (not missing)
  local -r z_label_check="${BURX_LABEL+declared}"
  test "${z_label_check}" = "declared" || buto_fatal "BURX_LABEL not declared in burx.env"
}

butcbx_burx_preexist_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: buf_write_fact_single rejects preexisting file"

  local -r z_stderr="${BUT_TEMP_DIR}/preexist_stderr.txt"

  # burx.env already exists from this dispatch's initial write
  local z_status=0
  buf_write_fact_single "${BUF_burx_env}" "duplicate-write" 2>"${z_stderr}" || z_status=$?
  test "${z_status}" -ne 0 \
    || buto_fatal "buf_write_fact_single should have failed on preexisting burx.env"
}

butcbx_burx_timestamp_format_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: BURX_BEGAN_AT matches nanosecond timestamp format"

  source "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}"

  # Format: YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN
  local -r z_pattern='^[0-9]{8}-[0-9]{6}\.[0-9]{9}$'
  [[ "${BURX_BEGAN_AT}" =~ ${z_pattern} ]] \
    || buto_fatal "BURX_BEGAN_AT format invalid: '${BURX_BEGAN_AT}' (expected YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN)"
}

# eof

  [[ "${BURX_BEGAN_AT}" =~ ${z_pattern} ]] \
    || buto_fatal "BURX_BEGAN_AT format invalid: '${BURX_BEGAN_AT}' (expected YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN)"
}

butcbx_multi_dual_write_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BUF multi: dual-writes <root>.<ext> to output and temp dirs with matching content"

  local -r z_root="butcbx_multi_a"
  local -r z_ext="probe"
  local -r z_filename="${z_root}.${z_ext}"
  buf_write_fact_multi "${z_root}" "${z_ext}" "alpha"

  test -f "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${z_filename}"   || buto_fatal "Missing temp dir copy: ${z_filename}"
  test -f "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_filename}" || buto_fatal "Missing output dir copy: ${z_filename}"

  local -r z_tmp_content=$(<"${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${z_filename}")
  local -r z_out_content=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_filename}")
  test "${z_tmp_content}" = "${z_out_content}" \
    || buto_fatal "Dual-write mismatch for multi: ${z_filename}"
  test "${z_tmp_content}" = "alpha" \
    || buto_fatal "Multi content mismatch: got '${z_tmp_content}' expected 'alpha'"
}

butcbx_multi_preexist_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BUF multi: rejects preexisting file on second write"

  local -r z_root="butcbx_multi_b"
  local -r z_ext="probe"
  local -r z_stderr="${BUT_TEMP_DIR}/multi_preexist_stderr.txt"

  buf_write_fact_multi "${z_root}" "${z_ext}" "first"

  local z_status=0
  buf_write_fact_multi "${z_root}" "${z_ext}" "second" 2>"${z_stderr}" || z_status=$?
  test "${z_status}" -ne 0 \
    || buto_fatal "buf_write_fact_multi should have failed on preexisting file"
}

butcbx_multi_empty_content_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BUF multi: accepts empty content (presence is the fact) and uses registry ext"

  local -r z_root="butcbx_multi_c"
  local -r z_ext="${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}"
  local -r z_filename="${z_root}.${z_ext}"
  buf_write_fact_multi "${z_root}" "${z_ext}" ""

  test -f "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${z_filename}"   || buto_fatal "Missing temp dir copy: ${z_filename}"
  test -f "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_filename}" || buto_fatal "Missing output dir copy: ${z_filename}"
}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUTCBX - BURX exchange test cases for BUK self-test
#
# Verifies that bud_dispatch.sh produces well-formed burx.env via
# dual-write during dispatch.  These cases inspect the current dispatch's
# own output — the dispatch IS the integration test for buf_write_fact_single.
# All tests are pure local — no GCP, no containers, no network.

set -euo pipefail

######################################################################
# Test cases — direct assertions inside zbute_tcase subshell

butcbx_burx_dual_write_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: burx.env exists in both output and temp dirs (dual-write)"

  test -f "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}"  || buto_fatal "Missing temp dir copy"
  test -f "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}" || buto_fatal "Missing output dir copy"

  local -r z_tmp_content=$(<"${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}")
  local -r z_out_content=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}")
  test "${z_tmp_content}" = "${z_out_content}" \
    || buto_fatal "Dual-write mismatch between output and temp dirs"
}

butcbx_burx_fields_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: burx.env is sourceable and has all initial fields"

  local -r z_burx="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}"

  # Verify sourceable in a subshell
  local z_status=0
  ( source "${z_burx}" ) || z_status=$?
  test "${z_status}" -eq 0 || buto_fatal "burx.env is not sourceable (status ${z_status})"

  # Source and check all initial fields
  source "${z_burx}"

  test -n "${BURX_PID:-}"        || buto_fatal "BURX_PID missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_BEGAN_AT:-}"   || buto_fatal "BURX_BEGAN_AT missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_TABTARGET:-}"  || buto_fatal "BURX_TABTARGET missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_TEMP_DIR:-}"   || buto_fatal "BURX_TEMP_DIR missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_TRANSCRIPT:-}" || buto_fatal "BURX_TRANSCRIPT missing or empty"
  test -n "${BURX_LOG_HIST:-}"   || buto_fatal "BURX_LOG_HIST missing or empty"

  # BURX_LABEL may be empty — verify it is declared (not missing)
  local -r z_label_check="${BURX_LABEL+declared}"
  test "${z_label_check}" = "declared" || buto_fatal "BURX_LABEL not declared in burx.env"
}

butcbx_burx_preexist_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: buf_write_fact_single rejects preexisting file"

  local -r z_stderr="${BUT_TEMP_DIR}/preexist_stderr.txt"

  # burx.env already exists from this dispatch's initial write
  local z_status=0
  buf_write_fact_single "${BUF_burx_env}" "duplicate-write" 2>"${z_stderr}" || z_status=$?
  test "${z_status}" -ne 0 \
    || buto_fatal "buf_write_fact_single should have failed on preexisting burx.env"
}

butcbx_burx_timestamp_format_tcase() {
  buto_trace "BURX: BURX_BEGAN_AT matches nanosecond timestamp format"

  source "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/${BUF_burx_env}"

  # Format: YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN
  local -r z_pattern='^[0-9]{8}-[0-9]{6}\.[0-9]{9}$'
  [[ "${BURX_BEGAN_AT}" =~ ${z_pattern} ]] \
    || buto_fatal "BURX_BEGAN_AT format invalid: '${BURX_BEGAN_AT}' (expected YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN)"
}

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUTT Testbench - BUK test framework self-test
#
# Exercises the BUK test framework (bute/butr/butd/buto) with kick-tires
# and BURE tweak cases.  Pure local — no GCP, no containers, no network.

set -euo pipefail

BUTT_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
BUTT_BUTS_DIR="${BUTT_SCRIPT_DIR}/buts"

# Source dependencies
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bute_engine.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/butr_registry.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/butd_dispatch.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bure_regime.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"

# Source test case files
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butckk_KickTires.sh"
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butcbe_BureEnvironment.sh"
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butcbx_BurxExchange.sh"
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butclc_LinkCombinator.sh"
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butcym_YelpModule.sh"

buc_context "${0##*/}"
zbuv_kindle
zburd_kindle

######################################################################
# Registration

butt_kindle() {
  butr_kindle

  # All fixtures are pure local
  butr_suite_enroll "self-test"

  # kick-tires fixture (2 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "kick-tires" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "kick-tires" butckk_false_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "kick-tires" butckk_true_tcase

  # bure-tweak fixture (9 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "bure-tweak" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_empty_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_both_set_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_name_only_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_value_only_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_name_too_long_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_value_too_long_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_label_valid_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_label_too_long_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_unexpected_var_tcase

  # burx-exchange fixture (4 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "burx-exchange" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_burx_dual_write_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_burx_fields_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_burx_preexist_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_burx_timestamp_format_tcase

  # buh-link fixture (3 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "buh-link" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "buh-link" butclc_link_osc8_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buh-link" butclc_link_fallback_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buh-link" butclc_link_variants_tcase

  # buym-yelp fixture (7 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "buym-yelp" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_cmd_resolve_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_link_osc8_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_link_fallback_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_ambient_preservation_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_fast_path_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_multi_markers_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_plain_mode_tcase
}

zbutt_noop_baste() {
  buto_trace "Baste (no-op)"
}

######################################################################
# Routing

butt_route() {
  local -r z_command="${1:-}"
  shift || true

  zburd_sentinel

  butt_kindle

  export ZBUTE_ROOT_TEMP_DIR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}"
  export BUT_VERBOSE="${BUT_VERBOSE:-0}"

  case "${z_command}" in
    buw-st)
      butd_run_suite "self-test"
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Unknown command: ${z_command}"
      ;;
  esac
}

butt_main() {
  local -r z_command="${1:-}"
  shift || true
  test -n "${z_command}" || buc_die "No command specified"
  butt_route "${z_command}" "$@"
}

butt_main "$@"

# eof

Stopped before tests. State summary:

## Files modified (8)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `Tools/buk/buf_fact.sh` | Rename `buf_write_fact`→`_single`; add `buf_write_fact_multi`; add `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` registry constant |
| `Tools/buk/bud_dispatch.sh` | Callsite rename (1) |
| `Tools/buk/burh_regime.sh` | `burh_list_capture` emits `<alias>.${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}` fact per profile (empty content) |
| `Tools/buk/buts/butcbx_BurxExchange.sh` | Callsite rename (4 incl. comments/messages) + 3 new multi-fact tcases |
| `Tools/buk/butt_testbench.sh` | Enroll 3 new tcases — burx-exchange fixture now 7 cases (was 4) |
| `Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` | Callsite renames (16) |
| `Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` | Callsite rename (1) |
| `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh` | Callsite rename (1) — required two reads (BBAAM committed Step 3 mid-flight) |

## Race-condition observed

- BBAAM committed Step 3 (`b010808d`, `rbgp_depot_unmake` reshape) while I was editing. My initial Edit on `rbgp_Payor.sh` failed with "modified since read"; re-read + retry landed cleanly.
- Final rbk callsites all live in governor-mantle / foundry-director territory — disjoint from BBAAM's depot-levy/unmake/list focus per its own Step 1 annotation. No semantic overlap.

## Decisions executed

1. ✓ Renamed `buf_write_fact` → `buf_write_fact_single` (28 callsites swept)
2. ✓ Emission lives in `burh_list_capture` (regime walker), not cli — capture is no longer pure (side-effect: fact emission). `burh_ssh_config` calls capture too, so it'll also emit alias facts on its dispatch — flagging this in case unintended
3. ✓ Empty content via `printf '%s\n'` (yields a one-byte newline file, mirroring `_single` semantics)
4. ✓ Three multi-fact tcases added to existing `butcbx_BurxExchange.sh`: dual-write, preexist rejection, empty content via `BUF_EXT_ALIAS`

## Remaining task (paused)

- Task #5: Run `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` and `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` — paused for parallel-chat coordination

Tell me when to run tests, or if you want to notch the work as-is first.
